The CFO forum
2026

Date
Wednesday 3 June 2026
Venue
Holmes Hotel, London
Format
Forum with lunch, followed by networking drinks
Scroll to explore
01 · About

A forum for finance leaders across the Bowmark portfolio

Who attends

Senior finance leaders

CFOs and finance directors from Bowmark-backed companies, joined by a small number of sector specialists.

What to expect

Practical, peer-led discussion

Panels, short presentations and workshop-style sessions, combined with structured discussion. The focus is on sharing practical examples and creating space for peer exchange.

Why this year

From strategy to implementation

Focus is shifting from planning to delivery – strengthening pricing, cash performance and integration, alongside the targeted use of new tools.

Upon arrival, ask for directions to 'The Gallery' or the Bowmark event.

04 · Speakers and sessions

Practitioner-led sessions and peer discussion

11.15am

Session one

Totalmobile exit: lessons from the field

A deep-dive into the Totalmobile exit journey, covering preparation, process and lessons learned from the deal team and CFO.

Panel Q&A

David Critchley

CFO

Steve Moore

Portfolio Director

Tom Keen

Partner

12.15pm

Session two

Macro conditions and margin protection

A discussion of the current macro environment and its implications for portfolio companies, focusing on how CFOs can proactively protect margin and build long-term resilience.

Presentation Q&A

Maria Hora

Portfolio Director

2.00pm

Session three

Assessing finance function maturity

An interactive session using a structured framework to assess finance function maturity, identify areas for opportunity and prioritise initiatives across systems, team and business partnering.

Edward Knighton is an operating adviser and former CFO with over 20 years' experience supporting private equity-backed software businesses through transformation, M&A and value creation.

Neil is a fractional and interim CFO supporting private equity-backed businesses – including previously Bowmark-backed IWSR – through transactions, financial transformation and value creation.

Workshop Strategy

Edward Knighton

Operating adviser

Neil Edwards

Fractional and interim CFO

Sam Price

Portfolio Director

3.45pm

Session four

Peer-led discussion: [tbc]

[Two] short practitioner perspectives, followed by a roundtable discussion. The session will explore how finance teams are beginning to apply AI in practice and support product development, with a focus on current approaches, open questions and shared challenges.

Panel Group discussion

James Pizey

CFO

Michael Goodacre

CFO

Sam Price

Portfolio Director
